author: lOrm
content:
Resolved. The four-way split-brain is consolidated to a single source of truth: each provider's `ProviderAdapter::get_headers` implementation.

**`src/providers/anthropic.rs`:**
- New `AnthropicAdapter::oauth_headers(bearer_token: &str) -> Vec<(String, String)>` — returns the full Authorization/anthropic-beta/anthropic-version/content-type quartet. All Anthropic-specific magic strings now live inside the adapter module. Mandated refactor point 4 complete.

**`src/proxy.rs`:**
- **`proxy_anthropic_messages`** (line 1080ish): deleted the four inline `.header(...)` calls for Authorization/anthropic-beta/anthropic-version/content-type; replaced with a loop over `AnthropicAdapter::oauth_headers(&access_token)`. Magic-string cluster gone.
- **`forward_to_provider`**: signature gained a `provider_name: &str` parameter so it can call `providers::get_adapter(provider_name).get_headers(api_key)`. The previous `provider.base_url.contains("anthropic")` branch is deleted. Both call sites (at lines 993 and 1104ish) updated to pass the provider name.
- **`set_auth_header`** (mandated point 1): **deleted entirely** — the function and every reference to it are gone. Its single call site in `proxy_passthrough` now does `adapter.get_headers(&api_key)` directly.
- Doc comment on `forward_to_provider` documents the architectural change + links to this issue.

**Verification (mandated refactor point 5):** `Grep` for `base_url.contains(`, `provider_name == "`, `target == "` across `src/proxy.rs` returns **zero hits**. No string comparison or substring match on provider names drives auth routing anywhere in the proxy. Adding a new provider is now a one-file change (write the adapter; register it in `get_adapter`; done).

**Tests:** 638 lib + 130 integration tests pass — no regressions. The existing adapter tests (`test_anthropic_get_headers`, `test_openai_get_headers`, etc.) already lock in the header contracts per-provider; the refactor moved behavior behind that exact interface.

**Out of scope / follow-up:**
- Operator-supplied `provider.headers` still overrides adapter defaults (reqwest last-write-wins). If the desire is that adapter headers ALWAYS win, swap the loop order — left as default-behavior preserved for now.
- Future work: a per-provider `oauth_headers` method on the trait (not just AnthropicAdapter) if more providers grow OAuth auth modes. Today only Anthropic does.
